# EU AI Act — High-Risk Obligations (excerpt)

> SYNTHETIC STAND-IN for local development and tests. Paraphrased excerpt, not
> the official text. Full high-risk enforcement applies from August 2026.

## Art.9 Risk management system

Providers of high-risk AI systems shall establish, implement, and maintain a
risk management system running across the entire lifecycle, requiring regular
review and updating as risks evolve.

## Art.10 Data and data governance

Training, validation, and testing data shall be relevant and sufficiently
representative. Providers shall examine datasets for possible biases that could
affect health, safety, or fundamental rights and lead to discrimination.

## Art.14 Human oversight

High-risk AI systems shall be designed so that they can be effectively overseen
by natural persons, including the ability to intervene or interrupt the system.
Consequential outcomes should not take effect without the opportunity for human
review.

## Art.15 Accuracy, robustness, monitoring

High-risk systems shall perform consistently across their lifecycle. Providers
shall monitor performance and disparate error rates across groups, and address
material degradation. Metrics and the methods used to measure them shall be
documented.
